heading: Mentor-protégé program
label: 475a Mentor-protégé program
notes: References in Text, referrred to in subsec. (h)(3), was in the original “section 317 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ()” and was translated as reading “section 371(a) of the Higher Education Act of 1965”, to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Section 1067q(a) of title 2020 U.S.C. 1067q(a)

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Department a mentor-protégé program (in this section referred to as the “Program”) under which a mentor firm enters into an agreement with a protégé firm for the purpose of assisting the protégé firm to compete for prime contracts and subcontracts of the Department.

children.heading: Eligibility
children.label: b Eligibility
children.text: The Secretary shall establish criteria for mentor firms and protégé firms to be eligible to participate in the Program, including a requirement that a firm is not included on any list maintained by the Federal Government of contractors that have been suspended or debarred.

children.label: A
children.text: A description of the assistance to be provided by the mentor firm, including, to the extent available, the number and a brief description of each anticipated subcontract to be awarded to the protégé firm.

children.label: B
children.text: A schedule with milestones for achieving the assistance to be provided over the period of participation in the Program.

children.label: C
children.text: An estimate of the costs to be incurred by the mentor firm for providing assistance under the Program.

children.label: D
children.text: Attestations that Program participants will submit to the Secretary reports at times specified by the Secretary to assist the Secretary in evaluating the protégé firm’s developmental progress.

children.label: E
children.text: Attestations that Program participants will inform the Secretary in the event of a change in eligibility or voluntary withdrawal from the Program.

children.heading: Approval
children.label: 2 Approval
children.text: Not later than 60 days after receipt of an application pursuant to paragraph (1), the head of the Office of Small and Disadvantaged Business Utilization shall notify applicants of approval or, in the case of disapproval, the process for resubmitting an application for reconsideration.

children.heading: Rescission
children.label: 3 Rescission
children.text: The head of the Office of Small and Disadvantaged Business Utilization may rescind the approval of an application under this subsection if it determines that such action is in the best interest of the Department.

children.heading: Program duration
children.label: d Program duration
children.text: A mentor firm and protégé firm approved under subsection (c) shall enter into an agreement to participate in the Program for a period of not less than 36 months.
With respect to an award of a contract that requires a subcontracting plan, a mentor firm may receive evaluation credit for participating in the Program.

With respect to an award of a contract that requires a subcontracting plan, a mentor firm may receive credit for a protégé firm performing as a first tier subcontractor or a subcontractor at any tier in an amount equal to the total dollar value of any subcontracts awarded to such protégé firm.
A protégé firm may receive technical, managerial, financial, or any other mutually agreed upon benefit from a mentor firm, including a subcontract award.

children.label: A
children.text: small business concerns;

children.label: B
children.text: small business concerns owned and controlled by veterans;

children.label: C
children.text: small business concerns owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans;

children.label: D
children.text: qualified HUBZone small business concerns;

children.label: E
children.text: small business concerns owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals;

children.label: F
children.text: small business concerns owned and controlled by women;

children.label: G
children.text: historically Black colleges and universities; and

children.label: H
children.text: minority-serving institutions;
describes the type of assistance provided by mentor firms to protégé firms;
identifies contracts within the Department in which a mentor firm serving as the prime contractor provided subcontracts to a protégé firm under the Program; and

children.label: A
children.text: an increase in the technical capabilities of protégé firms; and

children.label: B
children.text: an increase in the quantity and estimated value of prime contract and subcontract awards to protégé firms for the period covered by the report.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: g Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed to limit, diminish, impair, or otherwise affect the authority of the Department to participate in any program carried out by or requiring approval of the Small Business Administration or adopt or follow any regulation or policy that the Administrator of the Small Business Administration may promulgate, except that, to the extent that any provision of this section (including subsection (h)) conflicts with any other provision of law, regulation, or policy, this section shall control.

children.heading: Historically Black college or university
children.label: 1 Historically Black college or university
children.text: The term “historically Black college or university” has the meaning given the term “part B institution” in .section 1061 of title 20

children.label: A
children.text: has the ability to assist and commits to assisting a protégé to compete for Federal prime contracts and subcontracts; and

children.label: B
children.text: satisfies any other requirements imposed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Minority-serving institution
children.label: 3 Minority-serving institution
children.text: The term “minority-serving institution” means an institution of higher education described in .section 1067q(a) of title 201See References in Text note below.1

children.label: A
children.text: is eligible to enter into a prime contract or subcontract with the Department; and

children.label: B
children.text: satisfies any other requirements imposed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Small Business Act definitions
children.label: 5 Small Business Act definitions
children.text: The terms “small business concern”, “small business concern owned and controlled by veterans”, “small business concern owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans”, “qualified HUBZone small business concern”, “and small business concern owned and controlled by women” have the meanings given such terms, respectively, under . The term “small business concern owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals” has the meaning given such term in .2section 632 of title 15section 637(d)(3)(C) of title 15So in original. The opening quotation marks preceding “and” probably should precede “small”.2
